cityDNA.coreFantasy: A wood-elven old-growth city where sacred groves, temple platforms, archives, alchemical labs, lecture halls, and heartroot cathedrals turn living branches into civic order.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Thalewood is not a loose forest village; the uploaded images show living-branch architecture with records, labs, temples, public learning, and formal court authority.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Thalewood turns old-growth roots, moonlight refraction, sacred groves, ritual geometry, archive memory, controlled alchemy, and public scholarship into a forest civilization with institutions.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Oak, limestone, timber, antler details, arcane glow, sacred groves, hanging gardens, living canopy halls, temple platforms, archive towers, alchemy labs, lecture halls, and branch cathedrals.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Wood-elven culture where stewardship is civic, religious, scholarly, and administrative rather than purely natural.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Heartroots, living branches, sacred groves, moonlight, star maps, antlers, lantern monuments, copyist halls, formula archives, and lecture courts symbolize wisdom grown into law.

canonLocks: Thalewood is a mature wood-elven civic forest, not a loose forest village.
The Wonder is Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches.
The Beacon is Sacred Grove Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court.
The five primary districts are Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove, Religious / Temple Quarter, Records / Archives, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, and University / Lyceum.

Exact uploaded anchors include Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook, Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook, Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook, University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, and Observatory Court Nook.
provisionalCanon: Thalewood's strongest civic read is moonlit stewardship shaped into institutions.
The uploaded image and sidecar pass were folded into v2 fields without adding a new top-level image-analysis section.
rejectedReads: nature harmony without archives, labs, or university life
cautionFlags: Preserve exact uploaded sidecar anchor names.
Keep grove, temple, archive, alchemy, and university districts visible together.
The forest is civic infrastructure, not a backdrop.

environment.environmentalLogic: Roots, branches, canopy, moonlight, and grove paths are civic systems that organize worship, records, laboratories, teaching, and government.

forms: Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches
Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court
skyline: Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches as far wonder anchor
Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court as central authority
Sacred Grove Beacon as living forest signal
archive towers, temple platforms, alchemy labs, and lecture halls nested in old-growth structure

civilization.people: Wood-elven citizens move through living architecture as scholars, caretakers, ritual attendants, archivists, alchemists, and teachers rather than wilderness figures.
civilization.governance: Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court governs grove law, temple obligations, archive records, alchemical permissions, and scholarly testimony.
civilization.religion: Heartroot worship, sacred grove rites, moonlight refraction, processions, antler symbols, and living-branch cathedrals make stewardship sacred and public.
bodyLanguage: careful handling of books and glass

streets.appearance: Living branch streets and old-growth plazas connect sacred groves, temple platforms, archive towers, alchemy labs, and lecture courts with blue lamps and moonlit paths.
streets.movement: Movement follows root paths, canopy bridges, procession stairs, archive approaches, lab courts, and university clearings.
streets.lighting: Moonlit silver, green canopy light, blue civic lamps, and warm temple lanterns divide district identities.
streets.weatherEffects: Rain brightens roots, deepens bark, and turns the forest architecture reflective and alive.
streets.dayCycle: Grove care, temple duties, archives, labs, and public lectures fill daytime.
streets.nightCycle: Moonlit rites, archive lamps, formula work, and quiet observatory lessons dominate night.

housing.wealthy: Court and grove-adjacent homes grown into old branches with moonlit terraces and private study rooms.
housing.middle: Archivist, scholar, temple, and lab homes around root streets and living-wood work courts.
housing.poor: Small service dwellings along root margins, with shared lamps, repair paths, and proximity to public institutions.

districts.name: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove
districts.civicPurpose: Sacred ecology, public signal identity, and stewardship authority.
districts.visualIdentity: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, Living Canopy Hall Nook.
gameplayAssetNeeds: Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall

districts.name: Religious / Temple Quarter
districts.civicPurpose: Public ritual, processions, sacred teaching, and heartroot ceremony.
districts.visualIdentity: Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, Procession Stair Nook.

districts.name: Records / Archives
districts.civicPurpose: Public memory, copied truth, stewardship law, and historical continuity.
districts.visualIdentity: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook.

districts.name: Arcane / Alchemical Labs
districts.civicPurpose: Controlled research, formula keeping, and forest-magic experimentation.
districts.visualIdentity: Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, Formula Archive Nook.

districts.name: University / Lyceum
districts.civicPurpose: Public teaching, star navigation, civic interpretation, and scholarly continuity.
districts.visualIdentity: University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook.

wonder.name: Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches
wonder.symbolicRole: Living forest, civic memory, worship, and public stewardship made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: Far skyline cathedral of heartroots and living branches with moonlit arcane glow.
wonder.civicRole: Citywide rites, stewardship ceremonies, public vows, and living-branch festivals.
storyHooks: A heartroot branch grows toward the record the court tried to hide.

beacon.name: Sacred Grove Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public warning rooted in living stewardship.
beacon.visualRole: Beacon embedded in sacred grove halls, hanging gardens, and canopy paths.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates grove, temple, archive, lab, and university response.
storyHooks: A Beacon shift turns the forest itself into a civic alarm.

seatOfPower.name: Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Celestial knowledge and forest law made government.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central high court with star-map ceilings, living-wood supports, sealed archives, and public terraces.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Governs grove law, temple obligation, archive rulings, lab permissions, and scholarly testimony.
storyHooks: A star-map ceiling reveals a precedent the court has been walking under for centuries.

majorLandmarks.name: Temple Platform
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Temple platform with lantern monument, procession stair, antler details, and living branches.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Processions, public rites, and temple authority.
storyHooks: A procession stops where the roots refuse to part.

majorLandmarks.name: Archive Tower
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Archive tower with gold-seal monument, copyist nook, and living-wood approaches.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Public records, stewardship law, and copied truth.
storyHooks: A copyist hall preserves the original grove oath.

majorLandmarks.name: Alchemy Lab
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Blue-lit lab with formula archive, glass vessels, roots, and controlled arcane glow.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Research, formula control, and regulated experiment.
storyHooks: A formula archive proves the forest can be harmed by kindness.

majorLandmarks.name: Lecture Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Lecture hall with star navigation monument, observatory court, students, and moonlit canopy.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Teaching, public interpretation, and scholarly continuity.
storyHooks: A student asks the question the court cannot answer.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall, Sacred Grove Beacon, Hanging Gardens Monument, and Living Canopy Hall Nook.
S2 must show Religious / Temple Quarter Civic Hall, Temple Platform, Religious Temple Quarter Lantern Monument, and Procession Stair Nook.
S3 must show Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, and Copyist Hall Nook.
S4 must show Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Alchemy Lab, Blue-Lamp Monument, and Formula Archive Nook.
S5 must show University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, and Observatory Court Nook.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches, Sacred Grove Beacon, and Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court legible together.
Use oak, limestone, timber, antler detail, arcane glow, living branches, sacred groves, archive towers, alchemy labs, lecture halls, and moonlit canopy routes.
requiredVisualElements: Wood-Elven Heartroot Cathedral of Living Branches
Star Map Ceilings Veiled High Court
Nature Preserve / Sacred Grove Civic Hall
elementsToAvoid: forest backdrop separated from civic structure
